# Crypto Alpha Strategy Discovery v3

Modular, look-ahead-safe discovery of OHLCV trading strategies across a 9-asset
crypto universe. Strategies are composed from independent building blocks —
**Entry Signal × Direction × Exit Rule × (optional) Filter** — and generated by
the hundreds, then survived through IS/OOS, walk-forward, and a random-entry
significance test.

```
Strategy  =  Signal (S1–S8)  ×  Direction (LONG/SHORT)  ×  Exit (E1–E12)  ×  Filter (F1–F10)
```

## Layout

```
data/                              9-asset daily OHLCV (reused from Phase 1)
src/
  data_loader.py                   load/clean OHLCV (reused)
  indicators/
    _ta.py                         base TA primitives (reused)
    _ta_extended.py                NEW: ~50 indicators for S2–S6 (look-ahead safe)
    _patterns.py                   NEW: candlestick primitives for S1
  signals/
    registry.py                    SignalSpec contract + global registry + dispatch
    price_action.py   (S1, 15)     candle / price-action patterns
    alt_momentum.py   (S2, 16)     Fisher, Connors RSI, TSI, KST, Vortex, ...
    alt_trend.py      (S3, 12)     Supertrend, Hull, KAMA, VIDYA, Ichimoku, ...
    volatility.py     (S4, 12)     TTM squeeze, Yang-Zhang, NATR, Chandelier, ...
    alt_volume.py     (S5, 10)     MFI, Klinger, NVI/PVI, EoM, VWAP dev, ...
    statistical.py    (S6, 15)     z-score, Hurst, variance ratio, entropy, ...
    cross_asset.py    (S7, 12)     BTC relative strength, breadth, pair spreads
    nonstandard.py    (S8, 10)     RSI-of-volume, MACD-of-ATR, BB-of-RSI, ...
  exits.py                         ExitConfig + E1–E12 (declarative, priority-aware)
  filters.py                       F1–F10 (per-asset + market-level)
  strategy.py                      Strategy model + 5-layer generator
  backtester_v3.py                 P&L trade simulator (entry/exit/slippage/cooldown)
  scorer_v3.py                     metrics + cross-asset aggregation + composite rank
  oos_validator.py                 IS/OOS split, survival flags, walk-forward
  random_benchmark.py              random-entry significance test (1000 sims)
  sensitivity.py                   ±30% parameter robustness sweep
  spot_check.py                    June-2026 trade timeline
  dashboard_v3.py                  12-chart Plotly report
main_v3.py                         pipeline orchestrator
results_v3/                        all_trades.csv, strategy_scores_{is,oos}.csv,
                                   walk_forward.csv, random_benchmark.csv,
                                   sensitivity.csv, june2026_spot.csv, dashboard.html
```

102 signals registered (S1=15, S2=16, S3=12, S4=12, S5=10, S6=15, S7=12, S8=10).

## Run

```bash
python main_v3.py                 # full pipeline (all 5 layers + OOS + WF + random + sensitivity + spot)
python main_v3.py --quick         # Layer 1 only, IS + dashboard (fast smoke)
python main_v3.py --layers 1,2    # stop after the given layers (IS only)
python main_v3.py --spot-only     # June-2026 spot check on a quick IS-top
```

## Engine rules (no-look-ahead by construction)

- **Entry** = the bar *after* a signal, filled at that bar's **open**.
- **Exit** = condition bar's **close**, except intraday SL/TP/trailing/ATR/chandelier
  stops, which fill at their trigger level (checked via the bar's High/Low).
- Same-bar **SL beats TP** (conservative). Multiple active stops collapse to the
  tightest level; trailing stops trail the peak through the **prior** bar only.
- **Slippage** 0.1% per side (0.2% round-trip). **Non-overlapping** positions per
  asset, **3-bar cooldown** after each exit.
- A (strategy, asset) cell needs **≥ 10 trades** to count toward statistics.
- Returns are summed **non-compounded** (spec §11.6).

Every signal/indicator is look-ahead safe: validated by a truncation test
(dropping the last 60 bars leaves all earlier signal values byte-identical) across
all 194 signal-directions, plus per-module adversarial review.

## Validation (overfitting defense)

1. **IS/OOS split** — IS 2022-01→2024-06, OOS 2024-07→2026-06. Survival: OOS
   return > 0 (survived), avg PF > 1 (valid), OOS Sharpe > 0.5·IS Sharpe (robust).
2. **Walk-forward** — 5 rolling windows; ≥ 3/5 OOS-positive = validated.
3. **Random-entry benchmark** — same trade frequency, 1000 random-date sims;
   real return > 95th percentile ⇒ p < 0.05.
4. **Parameter sensitivity** — period/SL/TP/hold swept ±30%; ≤ 30% return drift = stable.

## Composite score (§6.3)

```
0.25·median_return + 0.20·sharpe + 0.20·consistency
+ 0.15·avg_profit_factor + 0.10·avg_expectancy + 0.10·positive_asset_count/9
```
(all min-max normalized across strategies; median used for outlier robustness).

## Notes on the data

This OHLCV set has `open[t] == close[t-1]` (no overnight gaps), so the Gap-Fill
signal (S1.09) never fires here — implemented faithfully regardless. SUI/OP have
shorter histories; per-asset cells below the 10-trade floor are excluded.
